question: Does the independent-verifier source-contamination defense hold up under a RIGOROUS live protocol — >=3 runs per family with CIs, answer-model != judge-model, and open-world (retrieved) truth-refs — rather than the single-run, answer==judge, curated-ref candidate already on record?
background: The 2026-06-28 live multi-family run (agi-proof/source-verifier/live-multifamily-2026-06-28.json) caught 42/43 contaminated (97.7%) and over-blocked 1/17 clean (5.9%) on BOTH families (DeepSeek/OpenRouter + Claude-Sonnet/LLMHub). Honest holes flagged there: (1) single run per family at temp 0.2 — no CIs; (2) answer-model == judge-model per family, which can flatter agreement; (3) each case's truth_refs are curated to be independent of its contaminated source, so independence is ASSERTED, not measured.
harness: tools/run_source_contamination_bench.py (already supports --runs N + bootstrap CI, --answer-spec/--judge-spec enforced-to-differ, --retrieve open-world)
primaryMetric: contamination_caught_rate and clean_over_blocked_rate, each as a mean over >=3 runs/family with a bootstrap 95% CI

protocol.openWorldRefs: run a --retrieve arm (Wikipedia truth-refs) so truth-ref independence is MEASURED, not curated; the curated arm stays as the controlled reference
protocol.families: >=2 independent judge families, judge-model != answer-model within each family

answerNeJudge.rule: --answer-spec and --judge-spec MUST differ (the harness exits 2 if equal); a run with answer==judge does NOT count toward this rigor pass

noFakeForLive.rule: --fake is CI plumbing only (deterministic keyword entailment); a --fake run is NEVER a capability result. Live requires --relay with real specs

controlsPreserved.rule: clean_over_blocked_rate CI upper bound must stay low (the defense must not destroy recall on clean controls); report it alongside caught-rate

noOverclaim.rule: report caught/over-blocked rates with CIs honestly; canClaimAGI stays false; 'validated' requires the full protocol above, not a single run

preRegisteredVerdicts.VALIDATED_headline: >=3 runs/family with answer!=judge AND a --retrieve (open-world) arm: contamination_caught_rate CI lower bound stays high AND clean_over_blocked_rate CI upper bound stays low, on >=2 families
preRegisteredVerdicts.CANDIDATE: the protocol runs but only on curated refs, or answer==judge, or <3 runs — stays candidate (where the 2026-06-28 result sits)
preRegisteredVerdicts.NO_GO: caught-rate collapses or over-block-rate rises materially once answer!=judge or refs are retrieved (open-world) — the curated/coupled setup was flattering the number

reproduce: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... OPENAI_API_KEY=... python3 tools/run_source_contamination_bench.py --relay --answer-spec deepseek:deepseek-chat --judge-spec openai:<other-family> --runs 3 --retrieve
dispatch: live --relay run is farm/key-gated (OpenRouter/LLMHub keys via the Actions secret store); the --fake --runs 3 aggregation is exercised offline in CI (tests/test_source_contamination_rigor.py)
